text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both professional and academic settings. Good time management starts with breaking large tasks into smaller, manageable steps and setting mini-deadlines for each. Prioritization is also key—identify which tasks are most urgent or important and tackle those first. Sometimes, you may need to practice scope management, which means adjusting the amount of work or the goals to fit the available time. Finally, don’t hesitate to communicate early if you foresee a problem with a deadline. Letting others know in advance allows for adjustments and reduces stress for everyone involved.
